title: Kuwait raises $6bln in bond sale after orders top $14.75bln
summary: Citi acted as the billing and delivery bank on the shortest tenor, followed by Goldman Sachs on the five-year and JP Morgan on the 10-year bond
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Kuwait City skyline and Al Hamra Tower, Kuwait. Image used for illustrative purpose. Image courtesy: Getty Images/ Murat Taner

Citi acted as the billing and delivery bank on the shortest tenor, followed by Goldman Sachs on the five-year and JP Morgan on the 10-year bond

Staff Writer ,  ZAWYA

Kuwait’s latest sovereign debt issuance raised $6 billion, with investor demand driving the combined orderbook to more than $14.75 billion across the three-tranche issuance.
